title: What My Sister Buried
theme: A town never forgets. It just redecorates.

cast.name: Mara Cross
traits: replies to texts while processing betrayal
owns a structured coat for crisis scenes
secret_capacity: has an unsent email that would destroy a family
buried one traumatic detail under work for years
cast.regional_responsibility: is trusted to lock up after emotionally charged events
cast.space_description: old storm shelter from before the divorce
cast.suspicion_profile: can host Thanksgiving but could move a body-shaped rug

cast.space_description: annex room described as just storage
cast.suspicion_profile: knows where a secret could fit architecturally

beat_sheet.text: Mara Cross relocates to Alder Ridge after an emotionally negligent implosion and pretends work explains everything while quietly inheriting old storm shelter from before the divorce. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Sienna Dane hesitates before explaining annex room described as just storage. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.00.

Elias Cross hates the move and immediately distrusts Sienna Dane, a regional dentist with suspiciously healthy handyman energy and access to annex room described as just storage. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.07. A basement light switches on in an empty house.

A wine scene at the gazebo at Lake Mercy introduces the blood-adjacent tea towel, local gossip about the Christmas pageant incident, and the fact that everybody here has somewhere to hide from a conversation. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.14.

Gavin Mercer reveals a perfectly phrased warning in a well-lit kitchen while bees in winter, then declines to explain meditation annex nobody uses in winter. Mara Cross hesitates before explaining old storm shelter from before the divorce. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.23. The ceramic owl is back on the kitchen island.

A cliffside emotional exposition reframes emotionally charged casserole exchange as a civic hazard and makes annex room described as old family things feel spiritually relevant. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.31.

Mara Cross snoops through a laptop, finds nothing conclusive, and becomes more certain anyway after she catches someone staring through a dirty window instead of answering inside old storm shelter from before the divorce. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Daniel Pruitt hesitates before explaining marina tackle room described as old family things. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.40.

The town offers a fake sense of safety through a fundraiser, a casserole, and a sheriff who suspects the wrong person first while ignoring annex room described as old family things. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.50. The sheriff sees fresh mud on the boat key.

Sienna Dane is accused, gaslighted, then gently framed near hydrangeas until the scarf with impossible timing turns up somewhere impossible inside annex room described as just storage. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.61.

A rain argument escalates into distributed secrecy infrastructure and the fake moral theme: 'A town never forgets. It just redecorates.' Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.72. The sheriff sees fresh mud on the boat key.

Someone goes missing after mentioning the unopened letter, the old orchard chapel, and meditation annex nobody uses in winter in the same sentence. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Sienna Dane hesitates before explaining annex room described as just storage. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.84.

The reveal ties boat key to Blackthorn Vineyard, St. Agnes Regional, and a brunch resurrection event by routing through annex room described as old family things instead of one simple basement. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.97.

Elias Cross spots the timeline error that every adult has spiritually rented around and asks why everybody in this county owns just storage. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.10. The sheriff sees fresh mud on the boat key.

A frantic drive toward Blackthorn Vineyard ends with ambulance lights, a one-line empowerment speech, and a locked door inside meditation annex nobody uses in winter. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Daniel Pruitt hesitates before explaining marina tackle room described as old family things. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.25.

Gavin Mercer confesses just enough to make the truth worse while the sheriff arrives exactly fourteen minutes too late and someone stares through the dusty window of annex room described as just storage. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.41. The sheriff sees fresh mud on the boat key.

Sequel bait lands when the ceramic owl reappears and someone whispers about the Winterson case, the vineyard fire, and another offsite room nobody has adequately explained. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.58. The ceramic owl is back on the kitchen island.

contradiction_ledger.entry: Sienna Dane is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
Mara Cross says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Marigold Falls.

dialogue_samples: "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Sienna?"
"Mothers do not disappear, Elias. They evaporate into paperwork."
"I checked his social media likes, Mara. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
"The blood-adjacent tea towel is not decorative anymore and you know it."
"Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Gavin."
"You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
"If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
"I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
fake_critic_blurb: "What My Sister Buried understands that menace is a regional utility." - County Suspense Quarterly
sequel_hook: Channel 8 Night Report hints that the vineyard fire had a witness who still hosts brunch.
synopsis: Inspired by true events in the emotional sense only, "What My Sister Buried" follows Mara Cross, an overextended professional woman who mistakes productivity for safety until emotionally charged casserole exchange collides with Sienna Dane, a comforting regional adult carrying portable suspicion and plausible access to offsite storage. As bees in winter, Blackthorn County reveals once again that every secret comes home and every adult has somewhere emotionally alarming to stand in silence. Inspired by something, probably.
